items.front: What entities must be notified for a ground missile emergency under 10-7-1?
items.back: EN ROUTE: Air carrier company radio stations for VFR company aircraft, and FSSs adjacent to the emergency location. TERMINAL: Relay all information to the ARTCC within whose area the emergency exists and disseminate as a NOTAM.

items.front: What are the IFR/SVFR separation minima from a ground missile emergency location under 10-7-2?
items.back: Lateral: 1 mile between the emergency location and the aircraft under radar control or the protected route airspace. Vertical: 6,000 feet above the surface over the emergency location. Greater minima if suggested by the notifying official.

items.front: What VFR separation minima apply for ground missile emergencies under 10-7-3?
items.back: Advisory: avoid by 1 mile laterally or 6,000 feet vertically. Greate distance or altitude when suggested by the notifying official. This is advisory, not mandatory separation.

items.front: What action is required regarding smoke columns near a ground missile emergency under 10-7-4?
items.back: Advise all aircraft to avoid any observed smoke columns in the vicinity of the emergency.

items.front: When may a NOTAM be issued for a ground missile emergency under 10-7-5?
items.back: EN ROUTE: When reports indicate the emergency will exist for an extended period of time, a Notice to Airmen may be issued.
